title: Hormuz disruption decision replay
disclaimer: This is an offline local demonstration assembled from simulated events and seeded historical route context. It is not connected to live AIS, news, prices, sanctions, insurance, ports, or tanker systems; it includes no external citations and must not be treated as an operational fact or procurement instruction.
replay_signal: Simulated signal: a hypothetical partial transit restriction near the Strait of Hormuz may interrupt India-bound crude cargoes over the next 21 days.

evidence.label: Scenario injection
evidence.summary: The local replay starts with an explicitly simulated partial disruption affecting the Strait of Hormuz corridor.
evidence.impact: Marks the Persian Gulf to West India corridor as exposed so the prototype can compare alternatives.

evidence.label: Seeded route baseline
evidence.summary: A seeded historical route profile identifies the Strait of Hormuz as a shared passage for the exposed corridor in this prototype dataset.
evidence.impact: Provides a labelled baseline for route exposure; it is not vessel telemetry or a current traffic observation.

evidence.label: Scenario capacity constraint
evidence.summary: The replay applies a 28 percent alternative-route capacity reduction and a 21-day disruption duration as visible scenario assumptions.
evidence.impact: Makes alternative cargo capacity finite so the procurement comparison cannot imply unlimited rerouting.

evidence.label: Analyst review gate
evidence.summary: A sample analyst assumption flags the simulated signal for review before any scenario result or portfolio can be relied upon.
evidence.impact: Keeps the replay evidence-first: user-entered context remains unverified and no external action is executed.

routes.name: Persian Gulf -> Strait of Hormuz -> West India
routes.note: Schematic exposed route for the local replay; it represents no live vessel location or current navigational instruction.

routes.name: US Gulf -> Cape of Good Hope -> Jamnagar
routes.note: Seeded alternative-route schematic with prototype capacity and risk assumptions; not an offer, booking, or live route plan.

routes.name: Brazil -> Cape of Good Hope -> Jamnagar
routes.note: Seeded alternative-route schematic with prototype capacity and risk assumptions; it is not live shipping or market data.
